name: Folate and one-carbon metabolism
summary: The tetrahydrofolate (THF) cofactor cycle that captures one-carbon units from serine and glycine and distributes them, in three oxidation states, to purine, thymidylate, and methionine (methylation) synthesis across cytosolic and mitochondrial compartments.
description: Folate (vitamin B9) is reduced by dihydrofolate reductase to tetrahydrofolate (THF), the universal carrier of activated one-carbon units. THF shuttles a single carbon at three oxidation levels — most-reduced 5-methyl-THF (methyl), 5,10-methylene-THF (formaldehyde-level), and most-oxidized 10-formyl-THF / 5,10-methenyl-THF (formate-level). Serine is the dominant one-carbon donor: serine hydroxymethyltransferase (SHMT1 cytosolic, SHMT2 mitochondrial) transfers its beta-carbon to THF to make 5,10-methylene-THF plus glycine; glycine itself is cleaved by the mitochondrial glycine cleavage system to release a second one-carbon unit. The methylene-THF pool then branches to three fates: (1) thymidylate synthase uses it to methylate dUMP to dTMP (oxidizing THF to dihydrofolate, which DHFR must re-reduce); (2) MTHFD1/MTHFD2 oxidize it to 10-formyl-THF, which donates formyl groups to purine ring atoms C8 (GART) and C2 (ATIC); (3) MTHFR reduces it irreversibly to 5-methyl-THF, which cobalamin-dependent methionine synthase (MTR) uses to remethylate homocysteine to methionine, feeding the SAM methylation cycle. In modern (Ducker–Rabinowitz) understanding the pathway is compartmentalized: mitochondrial serine catabolism (SHMT2, MTHFD2, MTHFD1L) generates formate that is exported to the cytosol and re-assimilated onto THF for biosynthesis, an axis strongly upregulated in proliferating and tumor cells. Two clinically central control nodes are the 'methyl trap' (vitamin-B12 deficiency traps folate as 5-methyl-THF because the MTHFR step is irreversible and only MTR consumes 5-methyl-THF) and antifolate chemotherapy (methotrexate on DHFR, 5-fluorouracil on TYMS, pemetrexed on TYMS/GART).

references.title: One-Carbon Metabolism in Health and Disease
Compartmentalization of Mammalian folate-mediated one-carbon metabolism
Metabolic enzyme expression highlights a key role for MTHFD2 and the mitochondrial folate pathway in cancer

compartments.name: Cytosol
compartments.description: Site of the DHFR/THF cycle, thymidylate synthesis, cytosolic serine one-carbon flux, and purine formyl transfers.

compartments.name: Mitochondrial matrix
compartments.description: Site of SHMT2, the glycine cleavage system, and the MTHFD2/MTHFD1L axis that generates and exports formate.

metabolites.name: Folate
metabolites.description: Fully oxidized dietary/supplemental vitamin B9; reduced twice by DHFR to the active THF carrier.

metabolites.name: 7,8-Dihydrofolate
metabolites.description: One-electron-pair-reduced folate; regenerated each turn of thymidylate synthase and re-reduced to THF by DHFR (the methotrexate target).

metabolites.name: Tetrahydrofolate
metabolites.description: The active carrier; accepts one-carbon units at N5 and/or N10.

metabolites.name: 5,10-Methylenetetrahydrofolate
metabolites.description: Formaldehyde-oxidation-level one-carbon unit; central branch point feeding thymidylate, methyl-THF, and the formyl pool.

metabolites.name: 5,10-Methenyltetrahydrofolate
metabolites.description: Cationic bridged intermediate between methylene-THF and 10-formyl-THF.

metabolites.name: 10-Formyltetrahydrofolate
metabolites.description: Formate-oxidation-level one-carbon unit; formyl donor for purine ring atoms C8 (GART) and C2 (ATIC).

metabolites.name: 5-Methyltetrahydrofolate
metabolites.description: Most-reduced one-carbon unit and dominant circulating folate; methyl donor for methionine synthase. The dead-end of the 'methyl trap' when B12/MTR is limiting.

metabolites.name: L-Serine
metabolites.description: Principal one-carbon donor; its beta-carbon is transferred to THF by SHMT. Derived from the glycolytic intermediate 3-phosphoglycerate.

metabolites.name: Glycine
metabolites.description: Product of SHMT and substrate of the glycine cleavage system, which releases a further one-carbon unit.

metabolites.name: Formate
metabolites.description: Diffusible one-carbon currency; exported from mitochondria (MTHFD1L) and re-assimilated onto cytosolic THF (MTHFD1).

metabolites.name: L-Homocysteine
metabolites.description: Methyl acceptor of methionine synthase; its accumulation (hyperhomocysteinemia) marks folate/B12 deficiency and MTHFR hypofunction.

metabolites.name: L-Methionine
metabolites.description: Product of homocysteine remethylation; precursor of the universal methyl donor S-adenosylmethionine.

metabolites.name: S-Adenosyl-L-methionine
metabolites.description: Universal methyl donor; allosterically inhibits MTHFR to couple methylation demand to one-carbon partitioning.

metabolites.name: S-Adenosyl-L-homocysteine
metabolites.description: Demethylated product of SAM; competes with SAM at the MTHFR regulatory site, relieving its inhibition.

metabolites.name: dUMP
metabolites.description: Substrate methylated by thymidylate synthase.

metabolites.name: dTMP
metabolites.description: Folate-dependent product of thymidylate synthase; the sole de novo source of the DNA base thymine.

metabolites.name: 5'-Phosphoribosylglycinamide
metabolites.description: Purine-ring precursor formylated at its amino group by GART (installs purine C8).

metabolites.name: 5'-Phosphoribosyl-N-formylglycinamide
metabolites.description: Formylated product of GART on the de novo purine pathway.

metabolites.name: AICAR
metabolites.description: Late purine intermediate formylated by ATIC (installs purine C2); as ZMP it is also an AMPK activator.

metabolites.name: FAICAR
metabolites.description: Formylated product of ATIC, cyclized to IMP to complete purine ring assembly.

metabolites.name: Orthophosphate (Pi)
metabolites.description: Also an allosteric activator of the mitochondrial MTHFD2 dehydrogenase.

enzymes.name: Dihydrofolate reductase
enzymes.description: NADPH-dependent reduction of folate to DHF and DHF to THF; the classic methotrexate/aminopterin target.

enzymes.name: Serine hydroxymethyltransferase, cytosolic
enzymes.description: Reversibly transfers the serine beta-carbon to THF, generating cytosolic 5,10-methylene-THF and glycine.

enzymes.name: Serine hydroxymethyltransferase, mitochondrial
enzymes.description: Mitochondrial isozyme initiating the serine-to-formate flux that dominates one-carbon supply in proliferating cells.

enzymes.name: C-1-tetrahydrofolate synthase, cytoplasmic (trifunctional)
enzymes.description: Trifunctional cytosolic enzyme interconverting methylene-, methenyl-, and 10-formyl-THF and assimilating formate (ATP-dependent).

enzymes.name: Methylenetetrahydrofolate dehydrogenase/cyclohydrolase, mitochondrial
enzymes.description: NAD+-dependent, Mg2+/Pi-activated bifunctional enzyme; among the most consistently overexpressed metabolic genes in cancer.

enzymes.name: Monofunctional C1-tetrahydrofolate synthase, mitochondrial
enzymes.description: Mitochondrial formyltetrahydrofolate synthetase running in the formate-releasing direction to export one-carbon units to the cytosol.

enzymes.name: Methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase
enzymes.description: FAD enzyme catalyzing the committed, physiologically irreversible reduction of methylene-THF to 5-methyl-THF; the common C677T variant lowers activity and raises homocysteine.

enzymes.name: Methionine synthase
enzymes.description: Cobalamin-dependent transfer of the 5-methyl-THF methyl group to homocysteine; the node linking the folate cycle to methionine/SAM and the site of the 'methyl trap' in B12 deficiency.

enzymes.name: Methionine synthase reductase
enzymes.description: Diflavin reductase that reductively remethylates oxidized cob(II)alamin to keep methionine synthase catalytically active.

enzymes.name: Thymidylate synthase
enzymes.description: Uses 5,10-methylene-THF as both one-carbon donor and reductant, converting dUMP to dTMP and oxidizing THF to DHF; the 5-fluorouracil/pemetrexed target.

enzymes.name: Cytosolic 10-formyltetrahydrofolate dehydrogenase
enzymes.description: Oxidizes 10-formyl-THF to CO2 + THF, providing an NADPH source and setting the size of the cytosolic formyl pool.

enzymes.name: Mitochondrial 10-formyltetrahydrofolate dehydrogenase
enzymes.description: Mitochondrial paralog of ALDH1L1; balances mitochondrial formyl-THF and contributes matrix NADPH.

enzymes.name: Phosphoribosylglycinamide formyltransferase (trifunctional GART)
enzymes.description: Its GAR transformylase activity uses 10-formyl-THF to install purine ring atom C8 during de novo purine synthesis.

enzymes.name: Bifunctional AICAR transformylase / IMP cyclohydrolase
enzymes.description: Its AICAR transformylase activity uses 10-formyl-THF to install purine ring atom C2, the last folate-dependent step of purine synthesis.

enzymes.name: Glycine dehydrogenase (decarboxylating) — GCS P protein
enzymes.description: PLP-dependent P protein that decarboxylates glycine and transfers the aminomethyl moiety to the H-protein lipoyl arm.

enzymes.name: Aminomethyltransferase — GCS T protein
enzymes.description: T protein that transfers the one-carbon unit from the H-protein to THF, forming 5,10-methylene-THF and releasing ammonia.

enzymes.name: Glycine cleavage system H protein
enzymes.description: Lipoate-bearing carrier that shuttles the aminomethyl intermediate between the P, T, and L proteins.

enzymes.name: Dihydrolipoyl dehydrogenase — GCS L protein
enzymes.description: Shared L protein that reoxidizes the H-protein lipoyl group, passing electrons to NAD+ (also a subunit of PDH/OGDH/BCKDH).

complexes.name: Glycine cleavage system (glycine decarboxylase complex)
complexes.description: Mitochondrial P/T/H/L multienzyme system that oxidatively cleaves glycine to CO2 + NH3, transferring one carbon to THF as 5,10-methylene-THF and reducing NAD+.

provenance.notes: KEGG R02236 is written in the oxidative (DHF->folate) direction; the physiological DHFR direction is reduction. Chiefly relevant to activating synthetic folic acid.
KEGG R00939 is written oxidatively (THF + NADP+ <=> DHF + NADPH); the physiological direction is DHF reduction. Regenerates THF from the DHF produced by thymidylate synthase.
Major cytosolic entry of one-carbon units into the folate pool.
Cytosolic MTHFD1 runs toward 10-formyl-THF, capturing mitochondria-derived formate for biosynthesis.
Physiologically irreversible; commits a one-carbon unit to methionine/methylation. FAD prosthetic group; NADPH is the reductant in vivo.
Cobalamin (methyl-B12) dependent. Only reaction that consumes 5-methyl-THF; its failure in B12 deficiency causes the folate 'methyl trap'.
Unique in the pathway: methylene-THF serves as both one-carbon donor and hydride donor, so THF is oxidized to DHF and must be re-reduced by DHFR.

reactions.name: 10-Formyltetrahydrofolate dehydrogenase (cytosolic)
reactions.equation: 10-Formyltetrahydrofolate + NADP+ + H2O -> Tetrahydrofolate + CO2 + NADPH + H+

provenance.notes: Overflow valve that returns THF and generates NADPH; sets cytosolic formyl-THF pool size.
Folate-consuming half of de novo purine synthesis (installs purine C8); full purine assembly is covered by the purine module.
Second folate-dependent purine step (installs purine C2); the accumulating substrate AICAR/ZMP is a physiological AMPK activator.
Initiates the mitochondrial serine-to-formate flux; SHMT2 is strongly induced in proliferating and tumor cells.

reactions.name: Glycine cleavage system
reactions.equation: Glycine + Tetrahydrofolate + NAD+ <=> 5,10-Methylenetetrahydrofolate + CO2 + NH3 + NADH + H+

provenance.notes: Overall P/T/H/L cleavage; a second mitochondrial source of one-carbon units. Loss-of-function of GLDC causes non-ketotic hyperglycinemia.
NAD+-dependent (distinguishing it from the NADP+-dependent cytosolic MTHFD1); Mg2+/Pi-activated. Key cancer-associated enzyme.
Cyclohydrolase activity of bifunctional MTHFD2, producing mitochondrial 10-formyl-THF.
Same KEGG reaction R00943 as cytosolic MTHFD1 but run in reverse: mitochondrial MTHFD1L releases formate (and ATP) for export to the cytosol.

reactions.name: 10-Formyltetrahydrofolate dehydrogenase (mitochondrial)
reactions.equation: 10-Formyltetrahydrofolate + NADP+ + H2O -> Tetrahydrofolate + CO2 + NADPH + H+

provenance.notes: Mitochondrial ALDH1L2 counterpart of foc10; buffers the matrix formyl pool and supplies matrix NADPH.

regulations.mechanism: S-adenosylmethionine binds an allosteric regulatory domain of MTHFR and inhibits it, so a high methylation charge shuts down further commitment of one-carbon units to 5-methyl-THF.
S-adenosylhomocysteine competes with SAM at the MTHFR regulatory site, relieving allosteric inhibition; the SAM:SAH ratio thus tunes MTHFR activity.
Methionine synthase reductase reductively remethylates the occasionally-oxidized cob(II)alamin cofactor of methionine synthase, restoring catalytic activity.
Mitochondrial MTHFD2 dehydrogenase activity requires magnesium and inorganic phosphate as allosteric activators, coupling matrix phosphate status to one-carbon oxidation.
Dihydrofolate (poly-gamma-glutamate) accumulating when DHFR is blocked inhibits AICAR transformylase (and thymidylate synthase), a mechanism contributing to antifolate cytotoxicity.

relations.note: Cytosolic (SHMT1) and mitochondrial (SHMT2) isozymes catalyze the same serine/glycine + THF interconversion in different compartments.
Methionine synthase couples the folate cycle to the methionine/SAM methylation cycle.
Homocysteine is the folate-cycle methyl acceptor and the branch point to transsulfuration; folate/B12 status governs its plasma level.
Thymidylate synthase provides the sole de novo route to dTMP, linking the folate pool to pyrimidine/DNA synthesis.
10-formyl-THF donates purine ring carbons C8 (GART) and C2 (ATIC) during de novo purine synthesis.
Serine that feeds one-carbon units is synthesized de novo from the glycolytic intermediate 3-phosphoglycerate.
Folate one-carbon oxidation (MTHFD/ALDH1L) is a major cytosolic and mitochondrial NADPH source, complementing the pentose phosphate pathway.
Mitochondrial serine-driven one-carbon flux (SHMT2/MTHFD2/MTHFD1L) is among the most upregulated metabolic programs in proliferating tumors; MTHFD2 is a leading anticancer target.
